level_signals: 7–12+ years of industry product management experience

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7–12+ years of industry product management experience (does not include internships or co-ops)

Experience in payments, fintech, financial infrastructure, or a closely adjacent domain (e.g., commerce platforms, banking, and payment networks) and a track record of building highly impactful products
Demonstrated experience partnering closely with engineers, designers, and external partners to build products and complex systems at scale
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a knack for precise and concise articulation of user problems

Strong analytical capabilities with experience defining and tracking success metrics, running experiments (e.g., holdback tests), and using data to measure product impact on revenue, conversion, and margins
Experience working on products that require aligning multiple stakeholders, developing joint roadmaps, and navigating competing priorities between internal goals and external partner objectives
Ability to thrive in a dynamic and fast-paced environment with significant autonomy and responsibility
Background delivering successfully on multi-quarter roadmaps and aligning and influencing diverse stakeholders to achieve shared goals
Strong business acumen and comfort with complex ecosystem and platform-level problems

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree (or relevant degree equivalent)—Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Analytics, Mathematics, Physics, or Applied Sciences
Demonstrated success and strong execution track record in building payment products
Experience working at scale—you've managed products handling high transaction volumes where availability, latency, and marginal basis points matter

scope.surface: Payments organization — subteams include payment APIs, regional/non-card payment options, in-person payments, Optimized Checkout, and Link
scope.business_model: B2B financial infrastructure platform (API-first, serving merchants and enterprises)
core_responsibilities: Develop the long-term vision and strategy for your pillar and create and execute on a compelling roadmap
Build deep user empathy with both Stripe's merchants and financial partners, understanding global payment industry trends and competitors' offerings to influence the Stripe roadmap effectively
Collaborate with engineering, design, legal, and other teams to jointly shape product experiences that delight Stripe's users
Ensure we are building a reliable, performant platform, including system reliability, API extensibility, and latency
Use metrics to inform your point of view and leverage analytics to measure success

green_flags: Clear ownership statement: 'own a critical piece of the Stripe payments stack'
Explicit team matching process described, with transparency about how it works
Candidates encouraged to apply if meeting minimum requirements
High-impact scope: products that directly affect Stripe revenue and millions of businesses
Well-scoped preferred qualifications are clearly labeled as bonus, not required
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated anywhere in the JD — unclear where role is based
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned
Team matching is deferred to final stages, meaning candidates may not know their exact team until late in the process
7–12+ years range is broad and the '+' qualifier makes the ceiling undefined

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7–12+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as API-first B2B platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location, remote, or hybrid policy stated in JD
compensation.equity_mentioned set to false — no mention found in JD body
ambiguities: Years range stated as '7–12+' — lower bound is clear (7) but upper bound is open-ended; minimum_value set to 7
Team matching deferred to final stages — candidates apply to the Payments org broadly and are matched to a subteam later; exact scope may vary
JD mentions possibility of being considered for 'different orgs based on your experience, location, etc.' — role placement is not guaranteed to be within Payments org
